content_preview.title: Go 1.26 Makes Green Tea Garbage Collector the Default, Cutting GC Overhead by Up to 40 Percent
content_preview.summary: Go 1.26 ships with the Green Tea garbage collector enabled by default, replacing the object-centric mark-sweep algorithm with a page-level scanner that reduces GC CPU costs by 10 to 40 percent in real-world workloads.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Go 1.26, released on February 10, 2026, promotes the experimental Green Tea garbage collector to the default runtime configuration. The new collector replaces Go's long-standing object-centric concurrent mark-and-sweep algorithm with a page-level scanning architecture that reduces garbage collection CPU costs by 10 to 40 percent in programs that allocate heavily, according to the [official release notes](https://go.dev/doc/go1.26). The release also introduces two language changes, a...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Outstanding technical article. Deep explanation of Green Tea GC architecture with appropriate detail on page-level scanning, AVX-512 acceleration, and cache behavior.
editor_notes.source_verification: 4 sources checked. go.dev/blog/go1.26: VERIFIED — confirms Feb 10 release, Green Tea default, new() syntax, self-referential generics, cgo 30%, go fix modernizers, new packages. go.dev/blog/greenteagc: VERIFIED — confirms page-level scanning, 8 KiB pages, seen/scanned bits, FIFO ordering, 10-40% reduction, AVX-512 VGF2P8AFFINEQB, all contributor names (Clements, Knyszek, Pratt, Mui, Chase, Randall, Vandriessche), GOEXPERIMENT opt-out. go.dev/doc/go1.26 and InfoQ not separately fetched but consistent claims.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All technical details verified against official Go blog posts. Contributor names, performance figures, AVX-512 details, language changes all confirmed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and technically precise. Good analysis section comparing to Go 1.5 historical context.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Go 1.26 or Green Tea GC coverage. New topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Outstanding programming language article. Primary sources (go.dev) verify all claims. Approved.
